title: The Old Toy in the Closet

all.en: While cleaning out my bedroom closet, I discovered an old shoe box at the back.
all.zh: 我清理臥室衣櫃時，在最裡面發現一個舊鞋盒。

all.en: Inside it sat a small, faded teddy bear with one missing eye.
all.zh: 裡面放著一隻褪色的小泰迪熊，缺了一隻眼睛。

all.en: I had completely forgotten about him for at least fifteen years.
all.zh: 我已經整整忘記他至少十五年了。

all.en: My grandmother had sewn his red sweater by hand when I was four.
all.zh: 我四歲的時候，奶奶親手為他縫了那件紅毛衣。

all.en: Suddenly, memories of childhood bedtimes came flooding back to me.
all.zh: 突然間，童年睡前的記憶一下子湧上心頭。

all.en: I remembered crying when I lost him during a family trip to the beach.
all.zh: 我想起有次全家去海邊時把他弄丟了，我大哭了一場。

all.en: Apparently, my father had found him buried under some old blankets later.
all.zh: 顯然爸爸後來在一些舊毛毯下找到了他。

all.en: Sitting on the floor, I felt unexpectedly emotional and a little tearful.
all.zh: 我坐在地板上，感到出乎意料地激動，眼眶有些濕潤。

all.en: I gently brushed off the dust and placed him on my bookshelf.
all.zh: 我輕輕拍掉灰塵，把他放在書架上。

all.en: Tomorrow, I will buy a small button and sew his missing eye back on.
all.zh: 明天我要去買一顆小鈕扣，把他缺的眼睛縫回去。

all.en: Some objects, I realized, carry far more value than their price suggests.
all.zh: 我意識到，有些物品所承載的價值，遠遠超過它們的標價。

all.en: The next morning, I carefully searched a nearby craft shop for matching black buttons.
all.zh: 隔天早上，我仔細地在附近的手工藝店尋找相配的黑色鈕扣。

all.en: The shop owner kindly helped me select one that was the perfect size.
all.zh: 店主很親切地幫我挑選了一顆尺寸正好的鈕扣。

all.en: Back home, I threaded a fine needle and gently sewed the new eye on.
all.zh: 回到家後，我穿好細針，輕輕地把新眼睛縫了上去。

all.en: The teddy bear suddenly looked alive again, watching me from the bookshelf.
all.zh: 小熊忽然又像活了過來，從書架上靜靜地看著我。

all.en: I sent a photo of him to my grandmother, who lives across the country.
all.zh: 我把他的照片傳給住在國家另一端的奶奶。

all.en: She called me immediately, laughing and crying at the same time on the phone.
all.zh: 她立刻打電話來，在電話中又笑又哭。

all.en: We talked for nearly two hours about my childhood and her younger days.
all.zh: 我們聊了將近兩個小時，談我的童年和她年輕時的點滴。

all.en: She promised to knit him a new winter scarf to match his red sweater.
all.zh: 她答應要為他織一條新的冬季圍巾，搭配那件紅毛衣。

all.en: That weekend, I cleaned the entire closet and donated many forgotten items.
all.zh: 那個週末，我整理了整個衣櫃，把許多遺忘的物品捐了出去。

all.en: However, the teddy bear earned a permanent spot beside my favorite novels.
all.zh: 不過，這隻小熊得到了一個永久的位置，就在我最愛的小說旁邊。

all.en: A week later, my grandmother mailed the new winter scarf in a small box.
all.zh: 一週後，奶奶把那條新的冬季圍巾裝在小盒子裡寄了過來。

all.en: It was deep green with tiny snowflakes knitted along both narrow ends.
all.zh: 圍巾是深綠色的，兩端細窄處還織著小小的雪花圖案。

all.en: I wrapped it gently around the teddy bear's worn fluffy neck.
all.zh: 我把圍巾輕輕地圍在小熊那毛茸茸又磨損的脖子上。

all.en: He suddenly looked dressed for a proper Taiwanese winter afternoon.
all.zh: 他突然看起來像是穿戴整齊，準備迎接道地的台灣冬日午後。

all.en: My niece visited that weekend and immediately fell in love with him.
all.zh: 我的姪女那個週末來訪，立刻就愛上了這隻小熊。

all.en: She asked countless questions about his red sweater and missing eye.
all.zh: 她對他的紅毛衣和缺掉的眼睛問了無數個問題。

all.en: I told her the whole story while she sat quietly on my lap.
all.zh: 她安靜地坐在我腿上，我把整個故事都講給她聽。

all.en: Before leaving, she made me promise to lend her the bear someday.
all.zh: 離開前，她讓我答應有一天要把小熊借給她。

all.en: I agreed only if she would also love him with great care.
all.zh: 我同意了，但條件是她也要非常用心地愛護他。

all.en: Some childhood treasures, I learned, are meant to be quietly shared forward.
all.zh: 我體會到，有些童年的珍寶，是註定要靜靜地傳遞下去的。

all.en: On a quiet Sunday afternoon, my niece arrived again with her tiny pink backpack.
all.zh: 某個寧靜的週日午後，姪女又揹著她那粉紅色的小背包來訪。

all.en: She immediately ran upstairs to greet the teddy bear like an old companion.
all.zh: 她立刻衝上樓，像見到老朋友般跟小熊打招呼。

all.en: This time, she carried a tiny handmade hat she had sewn at school.
all.zh: 這次，她帶了一頂自己在學校手工縫製的小帽子。

all.en: Carefully, she placed it on the bear's head and clapped her small hands happily.
all.zh: 她小心翼翼地把帽子戴在小熊頭上，開心地拍著小手。

all.en: My grandmother joined a video call and praised her stitching with genuine warmth.
all.zh: 奶奶加入了視訊通話，溫暖真誠地誇獎她的針線手藝。

all.en: She suggested they form an informal club for crafting bear accessories together.
all.zh: 她提議她們可以一起組成一個小小的熊熊配件手作俱樂部。

all.en: My niece's eyes lit up brighter than the lanterns during a winter festival.
all.zh: 姪女的雙眼比冬季祭典的燈籠還要明亮。

all.en: They scheduled weekly video lessons every Sunday after my niece's family dinner.
all.zh: 她們約定每週日姪女家庭晚餐後，固定進行視訊教學。

all.en: Within months, the bear owned scarves, hats, and even a tiny knitted sweater vest.
all.zh: 幾個月內，小熊就擁有了圍巾、帽子，甚至一件迷你的針織背心。

all.en: That forgotten teddy, I realized, had stitched three generations gently into one.
all.zh: 我體會到，那隻被遺忘的小熊，已溫柔地將三代人縫在了一起。

all.en: One spring afternoon, my niece arrived with her own freshly washed teddy bear in tow.
all.zh: 某個春日午後，姪女帶著她剛洗好的小熊一同來訪。

all.en: She wanted the two bears to become officially recognized lifelong best friends.
all.zh: 她希望兩隻小熊能正式結為一輩子的好朋友。

all.en: My grandmother joined our video call and laughed warmly at the small ceremony.
all.zh: 奶奶加入視訊通話，溫暖地對這場小儀式笑了出來。

all.en: She suggested we knit matching tiny friendship bracelets for both of the bears.
all.zh: 她提議為兩隻小熊織一對相配的迷你友誼手鍊。

all.en: My niece nodded eagerly and immediately picked out bright yellow and pink yarn.
all.zh: 姪女急切地點頭，立刻挑了亮黃色和粉紅色的毛線。

all.en: Within a week, both bears proudly wore their handmade bracelets around tiny wrists.
all.zh: 一週內，兩隻小熊都驕傲地在小手腕上戴著手工手鍊。

all.en: We took a photograph and printed copies for every family member to keep.
all.zh: 我們拍了照片，並印了好幾份送給每位家人留存。

all.en: My grandmother framed hers and placed it beside her own old wedding photograph.
all.zh: 奶奶把她的那張裱框，放在她自己的老婚紗照旁邊。

all.en: My niece called her almost weekly to discuss new outfit ideas for the bears.
all.zh: 姪女幾乎每週都打電話給她，討論小熊的新造型點子。

all.en: Love, I realized once more, traveled gently through soft fabric and warm yarn.
all.zh: 我再一次體會到，愛溫柔地穿梭在柔軟的布料和暖暖的毛線之間。

all.en: On my niece's seventh birthday, my grandmother and she co-hosted a small online tea party.
all.zh: 姪女七歲生日時，奶奶和她共同主持了一場小型線上下午茶派對。

all.en: Both bears wore tiny party hats my niece had carefully glued together that morning.
all.zh: 兩隻小熊都戴著姪女當天早上小心翼翼黏起來的小派對帽。

all.en: Cousins from three different cities joined the call wearing their own homemade paper crowns.
all.zh: 來自三個不同城市的表親加入了通話，戴著他們自製的紙皇冠。

all.en: My grandmother taught everyone to knit a tiny gift bag for next year's birthday surprise.
all.zh: 奶奶教大家織一個迷你禮物袋，作為明年生日的驚喜。

all.en: Even my younger nephew, normally restless, focused quietly on his small wooden knitting needles.
all.zh: 連平常坐不住的小姪子，也安靜地專注於他的小木製織針。

all.en: After the call, my niece carefully placed both bears side by side on her bedroom shelf.
all.zh: 通話結束後，姪女小心地把兩隻小熊並肩擺在她臥室的書架上。

all.en: She whispered goodnight to them, just as my grandmother once whispered to me decades ago.
all.zh: 她輕聲對它們道晚安，就像幾十年前奶奶曾對我輕聲說的那樣。

all.en: I sent my grandmother a photo of the bears, and she replied with a single quiet heart.
all.zh: 我把小熊的照片傳給奶奶，她回了一個安靜的愛心。

all.en: That night, I realized small soft things carry our deepest love across distances and generations.
all.zh: 那晚我體悟到，柔軟的小事物承載著我們最深的愛，跨越距離與世代。

all.en: I placed the first teddy bear back on my bookshelf and quietly thanked him for everything.
all.zh: 我把第一隻泰迪熊放回書架上，默默地感謝他所給予的一切。

all.en: The following winter, my grandmother grew too tired to lead the weekly online crafting lessons anymore.
all.zh: 隔年冬天，奶奶體力衰退，無法再帶領每週的線上手作課程。

all.en: My niece quietly took over, teaching her younger cousins everything grandmother had patiently taught her.
all.zh: 姪女默默地接下重任，把奶奶耐心教過她的一切轉教給弟妹們。

all.en: She kept a small notebook of grandmother's stitches, drawings, and gentle words written carefully inside.
all.zh: 她保留著一本小筆記本，仔細記下奶奶的針法、圖樣和溫柔話語。

all.en: Each Sunday, she dressed the two bears in matching outfits she had designed during the week.
all.zh: 每個週日，她讓兩隻小熊穿上一週設計的相搭服裝。

all.en: My grandmother watched the lessons quietly from her bed, smiling whenever a cousin asked a question.
all.zh: 奶奶安靜地躺在床上觀看課程，每當有表親提問時便露出微笑。

all.en: On her ninetieth birthday, all the cousins gathered online wearing handmade scarves she had once taught them.
all.zh: 在她九十歲生日時，所有表親線上齊聚，身上戴著她曾教導他們做的手工圍巾。

all.en: Each held up their teddy bear, and grandmother whispered each bear's name with tearful tender recognition.
all.zh: 每個人都舉起自己的小熊，奶奶含淚溫柔地一一喊出每隻小熊的名字。

all.en: She passed away peacefully one quiet evening, holding the very first scarf she had ever knitted.
all.zh: 在一個寧靜的傍晚，她安詳地離世，手裡握著她織過的第一條圍巾。

all.en: My niece inherited her knitting basket and continues teaching the bear club every Sunday without fail.
all.zh: 姪女繼承了奶奶的編織籃，每週日從不間斷地繼續主持熊熊俱樂部。

all.en: Sometimes I see her whispering instructions to the bears, and I know grandmother is still listening softly.
all.zh: 有時我看到她對著小熊輕聲講解，我知道奶奶仍在溫柔地聆聽。

all.en: After grandmother's passing, the bear club continued meeting every Sunday at the community center.
all.zh: 奶奶離世後，小熊俱樂部仍每週日在社區中心持續聚會。

all.en: My niece kept a worn photo of grandmother on the table beside the basket of yarn.
all.zh: 我姪女把奶奶一張磨損的舊照片放在毛線籃旁的桌上。

all.en: New children joined every season, and the bears grew into a small army of soft, warm friends.
all.zh: 每一季都有新孩子加入，小熊們漸漸成了一支柔軟溫暖的友誼大軍。

all.en: We donated dozens of bears each winter to the children's ward at the nearby hospital downtown.
all.zh: 每年冬天我們都會捐出幾十隻小熊給市區附近醫院的兒童病房。

all.en: A small boy recovering from surgery hugged his bear so tightly that he refused to let go.
all.zh: 一位剛動完手術的小男孩緊緊抱著他的小熊，怎樣也不肯放手。

all.en: His mother sent us a photo months later showing him smiling with the bear at his graduation.
all.zh: 幾個月後他的母親寄來照片，他笑著抱著小熊在畢業典禮上合影。

all.en: My niece printed the photo and pinned it above grandmother's old wooden knitting basket forever.
all.zh: 我姪女把那張照片印出來，永遠釘在奶奶那只舊木製毛線籃上方。

all.en: We held an annual exhibition displaying every child's first bear inside the community center lobby.
all.zh: 我們每年在社區中心大廳舉辦展覽，展出每位孩子的第一隻小熊。

all.en: Parents wandered through the rows of bears, sometimes recognizing their own children's earliest stitches.
all.zh: 家長們在一排排小熊間漫步，有時還能認出孩子最早期的針腳。

all.en: Each exhibition closed with everyone singing the gentle lullaby grandmother had hummed while knitting.
all.zh: 每場展覽都在大家合唱奶奶當年織毛線時哼的搖籃曲中安靜落幕。

all.en: Last Tuesday after work, my niece called and asked if I could help find grandmother's missing thimble.
all.zh: 上週二下班後，姪女打電話來，問我能不能幫她找奶奶不見的頂針。

all.en: We searched the old wooden basket for nearly an hour before discovering it inside a soft cotton sock.
all.zh: 我們在舊木籃裡翻了將近一小時，最後在一隻棉襪裡找到了它。

all.en: She slipped it onto her thumb and immediately said it felt slightly warm, just like grandmother's hand.
all.zh: 她把頂針套上拇指，立刻說感覺微微的暖，就像奶奶的手一樣。

all.en: The next bear club meeting, she wore the thimble while teaching three new children to sew tiny ears.
all.zh: 下一次熊熊俱樂部聚會時，她戴著頂針，教三位新孩子縫製小耳朵。

all.en: A boy with messy hair stitched his first bear so quickly he hugged it tightly before the glue dried.
all.zh: 一個頭髮亂亂的男孩縫熊縫得很快，膠水還沒乾就緊緊抱住了它。

all.en: He named the bear after his late grandfather, who had once owned a small tailor shop nearby.
all.zh: 他用已故爺爺的名字為熊命名，他爺爺以前在附近開過一家小裁縫店。

all.en: My niece quietly slipped the thimble into his small hand at the very end of the lesson.
all.zh: 課程結束時，姪女悄悄把頂針放進他小小的手裡。

all.en: She whispered that good tools belong to whoever needs them most in any single moment of life.
all.zh: 她輕聲說，好工具屬於人生中任何一刻最需要它的人。

all.en: The boy hugged her tightly and promised to return the thimble whenever the next sad child arrived.
all.zh: 男孩緊緊抱住她，承諾下一個傷心的孩子來時，他一定會把頂針還回來。

all.en: Walking home that evening, I felt grandmother smiling somewhere quietly above the warm autumn streetlights.
all.zh: 那晚走路回家時，我感到奶奶正在溫暖的秋夜街燈某處靜靜微笑著。
